title: Aftermath - Let's Play - Part Two
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4qtFClwSzc8

Part two opens with a rules correction: Daniel (@dungeondive) realises he played the previous video's defence wrong — defence must be *initiated* with a blue card to claim the colour bonus, not just any matching value. Grumpel quietly drops to one HP. The dice luck does not improve.

Across the rest of the chapter the party scrapes through the gecko fight (Mesaiya's toothpick crossbow finally lands the killing shot from across the street), then has to nest *twice* mid-mission to heal — burning food and, more painfully, burning *time* against the mission's clock. Two Insight spaces trigger lore vignettes (an abandoned red shoe; the rusted sedan leaking poison into a puddle); the puddle adds **Toxic Water** to the mission deck via the discovery system. The chapter also surfaces Aftermath's component-shipping hiccup: Daniel's copy arrived with two Ringer minis and no Meekling, so he proxies.

On reaching Enjo the heroes face a fresh Meekling Nomad warband; Grumpel and Mesaiya kill both, gain scrap, and trigger a Group Task: ten points of strength (contributed across multiple turns and characters) to shift the rubble blocking the vending machine's coin slot. Once cleared, each contributing hero adds their **Influence card** into the campaign deck — a small permanent character-flavour upgrade that will reshuffle into every future draw.

----

Aftermath's Group Tasks let multiple heroes contribute action cards across multiple turns toward one collective skill check. Where else have you seen a co-op mechanic let players bank progress like that — and does it work?
